151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}
187	
188	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}

1	\chapter{Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form; Mumford \S4 in the abelian-variety case)}
2	\label{chap:Rigidity}
3	
4	This chapter records a self-contained helper: \emph{rigidity for morphisms of schemes that agree on a non-empty open}. The classical motivation is Mumford's rigidity for abelian-variety morphisms, but the project's formalised statement is more general: the source-side group-object hypothesis is unused by the proof and is dropped.
5	
6	The lemma is independent of the construction of $\Jac(C)$. It is the load-bearing input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0 Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex \S C.2.b), where the source is taken to be $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (which is \emph{not} a group scheme).
7	
8	\section{Statement}
9	
10	\begin{theorem}\leanok
11	[Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)]
12	  \label{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}
13	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen}
14	  Let $k$ be a field. Let $X$ be a reduced geometrically irreducible $k$-scheme and let $Y$ be a separated $k$-scheme. Let $g_1, g_2 \colon X \to Y$ be two morphisms of $k$-schemes, and let $U \subseteq X$ be a non-empty open subset. If
15	  \[
16	    U.\iota \circ g_1 \;=\; U.\iota \circ g_2
17	  \]
18	  as scheme morphisms (where $U.\iota \colon U \hookrightarrow X$ is the canonical open immersion), then $g_1 = g_2$ as morphisms of $k$-schemes.
19	\end{theorem}
20	
21	\begin{remark}
22	  The classical Mumford rigidity theorem (\emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S4) usually states the special case where $Y$ is an abelian variety and the conclusion is phrased as ``a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero''. The two forms are interderivable in the abelian-variety case via the difference morphism $\delta = g_1 \cdot g_2^{-1}$ formed from the group law on $Y$. The project formalises the more general statement above because the M2.a (genus-0 Albanese-witness) consumer specifically needs the source to be $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, which is not a group scheme: the abelian-variety packaging is consumer-side and the scheme-level statement is what the proof actually establishes.
23	
24	  The refactor that landed the scheme-level form (iter-125; see \texttt{analogies/rigidity-refactor.md}) dropped 8 unused hypotheses from the project's original group-scheme-flavoured declaration and weakened the target-side $[\mathtt{IsProper}\ Y.\mathrm{hom}]$ to $[\mathtt{IsSeparated}\ Y.\mathrm{hom}]$.
25	\end{remark}
26	
27	\section{Proof sketch}
28	
29	\begin{proof}
30	  \leanok
31	  We reduce to Mathlib's \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated}), which gives the standard scheme-level fact that two morphisms from a reduced scheme to a separated scheme agreeing on a dominant subscheme agree everywhere.
32	
33	  The three load-bearing instances feeding that closure are:
34	  \begin{itemize}
35	    \item \emph{$Y \to \Spec k$ is separated.} Hypothesis $[\mathtt{IsSeparated}\ Y.\mathrm{hom}]$ provides this directly (no projection from $\mathtt{IsProper}$ needed). The \texttt{OverClass.fromOver} instance transports it to the form $\mathtt{IsSeparated}\ (Y.\mathrm{left} \downarrow \Spec k)$ that the closure step expects.
36	    \item \emph{$X$ is an irreducible space.} Hypothesis $[\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\ X.\mathrm{hom}]$ over the field-spectrum base $\Spec k$ (which is a single point, hence subsingleton) yields $\mathtt{IrreducibleSpace}\ X.\mathrm{left}$ via \texttt{GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace\_of\_subsingleton}.
37	    \item \emph{The inclusion $U.\iota$ is dominant.} A non-empty open in an irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant: \texttt{Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant\_ι}.
38	  \end{itemize}
39	  With these three derived instances and the hypothesis $[\mathtt{IsReduced}\ X.\mathrm{left}]$, the closure \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} produces the underlying scheme-morphism equality $g_1.\mathrm{left} = g_2.\mathrm{left}$ from the agreement-on-$U$ hypothesis. The promotion to $g_1 = g_2$ in $\mathrm{Over}\ (\Spec k)$ uses \texttt{Over.OverMorphism.ext}.
40	
41	  \medskip
42	  \noindent\textbf{Mathlib ingredients.}
43	  \begin{itemize}
44	    \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} for the scheme-level closure step.
45	    \item \texttt{GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace\_of\_subsingleton} to derive irreducibility of $X.\mathrm{left}$.
46	    \item \texttt{Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant\_ι} to derive dominance of the open immersion $U.\iota$ from the non-empty hypothesis.
47	    \item \texttt{Over.OverMorphism.ext} to promote underlying scheme equality to $\mathrm{Over}$-morphism equality.
48	    \item No abelian-variety / group-object infrastructure is needed: smoothness, properness, and group-object hypotheses on $X$ and $Y$ are all dropped from the formalised statement.
49	  \end{itemize}
50	\end{proof}
51	
52	\section{Use in the project}
53	
54	The rigidity theorem is used in two places downstream:
55	
56	\begin{itemize}
57	  \item \textbf{M2.a (genus-0 Albanese witness).} Jacobian.tex \S C.2.b reduces the genus-0 rigidity-over-$\bar k$ argument (for $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A_{\bar k}$ hitting the identity at a $\bar k$-point) to this theorem with $X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ and $Y := A_{\bar k}$. The refactor of iter-125 (dropping the source-side group-object hypothesis) is what enables this application. The named project declaration packaging the C.2.a–C.2.e reduction is \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} in \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}; that chapter scaffolds the iter-126 Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar} with the C.2.d cotangent-vanishing keystone residual.
58	  \item \textbf{Uniqueness in the Albanese property.} Used in the uniqueness half of Theorem~\ref{thm:exists_unique_ofCurve_comp} (the Albanese property of $\Jac(C)$): given two morphisms $g_1, g_2 \colon \Jac(C) \to A$ with $g_1 \circ \alpha_P = g_2 \circ \alpha_P$, scheme-level agreement on the image of $\alpha_P$ (a non-empty open after passing to $C^{(g)} \twoheadrightarrow \Jac(C)$) forces $g_1 = g_2$.
59	\end{itemize}
60	
61	Note: the existence half of \ref{thm:exists_unique_ofCurve_comp} requires Phase~B/C infrastructure (Picard scheme, dual abelian variety). Rigidity alone yields \emph{uniqueness} only.
62	
63	\section{Mathlib status}
64	
65	The scheme-level form is a thin specialization of Mathlib's existing \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated}); the project's contribution is the $\mathrm{Over}\ (\Spec k)$-bundled packaging plus the convenience derivations of \texttt{IrreducibleSpace} and \texttt{IsDominant} from the project-side hypotheses.
66	
67	An upstream-PR candidate is a thin corollary in Mathlib's \texttt{Separated} file:
68	\[
69	  \texttt{ext\_of\_eqOnNonemptyOpen} \;\colon\; \texttt{(non-empty open) + (reduced source) + (separated target) }\Rightarrow\texttt{ ext}
70	\]
71	packaging the dense-and-dominant chain into a single API call. The incremental value over the existing \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} is small (just the API convenience of "non-empty open" over "dominant morphism"); see \texttt{analogies/rigidity-refactor.md} for the proposed statement and home file. The project does not pursue this upstream PR as part of the iter-125 work.

230	  Mathematically this reversal is justified because the underlying scheme of any Albanese object is intrinsic to $C$: a different choice of $P \in C(k)$ alters the universal morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to J$ by a translation of $J$, which is an automorphism of $J$, and so leaves $J$ unchanged as a scheme. The reversed bundling is what makes the per-$P$ projection in Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} clean: $\mathtt{Jacobian.ofCurve}$, $\mathtt{Jacobian.comp\_ofCurve}$, and $\mathtt{Jacobian.exists\_unique\_ofCurve\_comp}$ each take a marked point $P$ as input and project the corresponding component of $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}\,P$ via the extraction trio (Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese_ofCurve}, Lemmas~\ref{lem:IsAlbanese_comp_ofCurve}, \ref{lem:IsAlbanese_exists_unique_ofCurve_comp}).
231	\end{remark}
232	
233	\begin{remark}
234	  \label{rem:JacobianWitness_smooth_redundancy}
235	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness}
236	  The fields $\mathtt{smooth}$ and $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ of an Albanese witness are mathematically redundant: the relative-dimension version implies the unqualified version via Mathlib's \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth}. Keeping both fields is a Lean-level convenience: $\mathtt{smooth}$ is the form required by the typeclass binder of \texttt{IsAlbanese} (and by the protected $\mathtt{Jacobian.instGrpObj}$ projection), whereas $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is the form required by the protected $\mathtt{Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_genus}$ projection. The redundancy carries no mathematical cost (the two fields agree) and no soundness cost (deriving one from the other would only re-run the same Mathlib instance synthesis at the projection sites).
237	\end{remark}
238	
239	\begin{theorem}\leanok
240	[Existence of an Albanese variety uniformly over the marked point]
241	  \label{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}
242	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.nonempty_jacobianWitness}
243	  % Cycle-fix (iter-149 plan-agent): drop the spurious \uses{def:Jacobian}
244	  % from this statement-block. Mathematically `def:Jacobian` is the entity
245	  % that depends on this existence theorem (def:Jacobian's \uses{...} lists
246	  % thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness), not the other way around. Listing
247	  % def:Jacobian here as a hypothesis of the existence theorem introduced
248	  % a 2-cycle in plastex's depgraph (def:Jacobian ↔ thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness)
249	  % that crashes `leanblueprint web` with RecursionError in depgraph.ancestors.
250	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus, def:JacobianWitness}
251	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field $k$. There exists a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $J$ over $k$ of relative dimension $g(C)$ such that, for every $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$, the scheme $J$ is an Albanese object for the pointed curve $(C, P)$ in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese}. Equivalently, the type of Albanese witnesses for $C$ (Definition~\ref{def:JacobianWitness}) is non-empty.
252	\end{theorem}
253	
254	\begin{proof}
255	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:genusZeroWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
256	  \leanok
257	  The proof is split into three classical constructions plus the genus-$0$ sub-case absorbed into the same witness (the latter packaged as the explicit construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}, which routes through \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} directly over $k$ per the iter-127 over-k commitment). Each route produces the same intrinsic scheme $J$; only the universal morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to J$ varies with the marked point $P$. We outline each route and name the Mathlib infrastructure each one requires.
258	
259	  \paragraph{(0) Reduction to a $P$-independent witness.}
260	  The witness $J$ depends only on $C$, not on the marked point $P$. Indeed, both classical constructions below produce $J$ as an intrinsic object of $C$ (the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$, or the Stein-factorisation target of $\Sym^g(C) \to \Pic^g_{C/k}$ translated by a fixed $P_0$); a different choice of $P$ in $C(k)$ alters $\iota_P$ by a translation of $J$, which is an automorphism, and so does not change $J$ as a scheme. The Lean encoding reflects this by bundling $J$ as a single field and the universal-property data as $\forall P,\, \mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, J$. We now turn to producing $J$.
261	
262	  \paragraph{Route A --- Picard scheme.}
263	  This route follows Hartshorne~III.4 and FGA Explained, Chapter~9.
264	
265	  \begin{itemize}
266	    \item[\textbf{(A.1)}] \emph{The relative Picard functor.} For a $k$-scheme $T$, write $C_T := C \times_k T$ and let $\pi \colon C_T \to T$ be the projection. The relative Picard functor is
267	    \[
268	      \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k} \colon (\Sch/k)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \Sets, \qquad
269	      T \longmapsto \Pic(C_T) / \pi^*\Pic(T).
270	    \]
271	    A $T$-point is a line bundle on $C_T$ modulo a line bundle pulled back from $T$. The set-valued formulation suffices for representability; the canonical abelian-group structure on $\Pic(C_T)$ descends to the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ and gives $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ a natural group-functor refinement.
272	
273	    \item[\textbf{(A.2)}] \emph{Representability.} For $C$ smooth proper geometrically connected over $k$ with a $k$-rational point, $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ (after \'etale sheafification, if $k$ is not algebraically closed) is representable by a $k$-group scheme $\Pic_{C/k}$, locally of finite type. This is FGA's main representability theorem (Grothendieck, FGA~232); the smooth-proper-geometrically-connected hypothesis ensures that the cohomology-and-base-change machinery applies and that $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = k$, which is needed for the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ to be \'etale-locally injective.
274	
275	    \item[\textbf{(A.3)}] \emph{The identity component.} Let $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ be the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$. As the identity component of a $k$-group scheme locally of finite type, $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is geometrically irreducible; for $C$ smooth proper of genus $g$, it is moreover a smooth proper $k$-group scheme of dimension $g$. Smoothness comes from the deformation-theoretic identification of its tangent space at the identity with $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ and homogeneity of the group action; properness from the fact that the natural map $\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}$ is an open and closed immersion onto a proper component (a consequence of the cohomological criterion: $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is the kernel of the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$, which is locally constant). Take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$.
276	
277	    \item[\textbf{(A.4)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi universal morphism.} For each $P \in C(k)$, the Abel--Jacobi morphism
278	    \[
279	      \iota_P \colon C \to \Pic^0_{C/k}, \qquad
280	      Q \longmapsto [\mathcal O_C(Q - P)]
281	    \]
282	    sends $P$ to the identity of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$. It is universal among morphisms $f \colon C \to A$ to smooth proper geometrically irreducible $k$-group schemes $A$ with $f(P) = \eta_A$: this is the classical Albanese property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, proved by reducing to the case $k = \overline k$ (Galois descent), where it is a standard consequence of the autoduality of the Jacobian and the fact that line bundles on $C \times A$ trivialised along $\{P\} \times A$ correspond bijectively to morphisms $C \to A^{\vee} = A$ sending $P$ to $\eta_A$.
283	  \end{itemize}
284	
285	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route A.} Each sub-step is missing.
286	  \begin{itemize}
287	    \item Sub-step A.1 requires a working theory of relative line bundles on a product $C \times_k T$ and the descent of the abelian-group structure on $\Pic$ along $\pi^*$. The pieces are present individually but not assembled.
288	    \item Sub-step A.2 requires FGA representability for $\Pic_{C/k}$, which in turn requires Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure not in Mathlib (\texttt{HilbertScheme}, \texttt{QuotScheme}, and the cohomology-and-base-change for proper morphisms with finitely presented kernels).
289	    \item Sub-step A.3 requires connectedness machinery for group schemes locally of finite type, the identity-component construction $G^0 \subseteq G$, and the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$.
290	    \item Sub-step A.4 requires the universal property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, which is the classical Albanese functoriality and is not in Mathlib in this form.
291	  \end{itemize}
292	
293	  \paragraph{Route B --- Symmetric powers and Stein factorisation (historical alternative; not pursued).}
294	  \textbf{Iter-144 disposition.} Route B is a historical alternative \emph{not pursued} by the project. It is preserved here for scholarly context only. Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA --- is the committed M3 route per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition. The decomposition, gating Mathlib pieces, and midpoint LOC figure ($\sim 9000$ LOC) recorded below are the iter-123 audit values (\texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}); the chapter does not re-cost Route B under current Mathlib snapshots.
295	
296	  This route follows Milne's \emph{Abelian Varieties} (Chapter~III) and avoids FGA representability at the cost of requiring quotients of schemes by finite group actions.
297	
298	  \begin{itemize}
299	    \item[\textbf{(B.1)}] \emph{The $g$-fold symmetric power.} Form the product $C^g$ and let $S_g$ act by permutation of the factors. The categorical quotient $C^{(g)} := C^g / S_g$ exists as a $k$-scheme, smooth and proper over $k$ of dimension $g$. Smoothness follows because the action of $S_g$ on the smooth $k$-scheme $C^g$ is free outside the diagonal locus and the local-ring computation at the diagonal points produces a smooth ring (this is the symmetric-product computation of Fogarty / Mumford). Properness descends from properness of $C^g$ via the geometric-quotient property.
300	
301	    \item[\textbf{(B.2)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi morphism on symmetric powers.} Fix a $k$-rational point $P_0 \in C(k)$. The map
302	    \[
303	      \sigma \colon C^{(g)} \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}, \qquad
304	      \sum_{i=1}^g [Q_i] \longmapsto \mathcal O_C\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{i=1}^g Q_i\bigr)
305	    \]
306	    is well-defined (the right-hand side is symmetric in the $Q_i$ and depends only on the unordered tuple) and is surjective. By Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, $\sigma$ is birational onto its image: a generic divisor of degree $g$ on $C$ has $h^0(\mathcal O_C(D)) = 1$, so the fibre $\sigma^{-1}([\mathcal O_C(D)])$ is a point at generic divisors; the special fibres are projective spaces $\mathbb P(h^0 - 1)$ over the locus where $h^0 > 1$.
307	
308	    \item[\textbf{(B.3)}] \emph{The Stein factorisation.} Because $\sigma$ is a proper surjection with geometrically connected fibres (each fibre $\mathbb P^{h^0-1}_k$ is geometrically irreducible), its Stein factorisation
309	    \[
310	      C^{(g)} \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_*\;} \Spec_{\Pic^g_{C/k}}\bigl(\sigma_* \mathcal O_{C^{(g)}}\bigr) \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}
311	    \]
312	    presents the second arrow as a finite morphism whose source is the intermediate scheme. Since the fibres of $\sigma$ are connected, this intermediate scheme equals $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ scheme-theoretically (the Stein factorisation is trivial on the second arrow when fibres are connected, by Stein's classical theorem). The middle scheme is therefore $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ itself, which is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of dimension $g$. Translating by $-g P_0$ yields $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, and we take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as in Route A. The universal property is then established as in Sub-step A.4.
313	  \end{itemize}
314	
315	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route B.} Each sub-step is blocked by a different missing piece.
316	  \begin{itemize}
317	    \item Sub-step B.1 requires symmetric powers of schemes (the construction $\Sym^g$ for a $k$-scheme of finite type) and finite-group-scheme quotients of schemes (the quotient $C^g / S_g$). Mathlib has finite-group quotients of types and of various algebraic structures, but not yet a usable scheme-level quotient by a finite group action with the smooth-target property.
318	    \item Sub-step B.2 requires Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, including the relative version for families of curves --- the cohomology-and-base-change machinery for proper morphisms.
319	    \item Sub-step B.3 requires the Stein factorisation theorem for proper morphisms of locally Noetherian schemes ($f \colon X \to Y$ proper $\implies$ $f_* \mathcal O_X$ is a coherent $\mathcal O_Y$-algebra). Mathlib has partial coherence-theoretic infrastructure but does not yet have the cohomology of $\mathcal O$-modules of finite type along proper morphisms in the form needed.
320	  \end{itemize}
321	
322	  \paragraph{Genus-$0$ sub-case (absorbed into the same witness).}
323	  When $g(C) = 0$ the witness's underlying scheme is $J = \Spec k$, and the universal property reduces to a rigidity statement.
324	
325	  \begin{itemize}
326	    \item[\textbf{(C.1)}] \emph{Brauer--Severi identification.} A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C$ over $k$ with $g(C) = 0$ and a $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_k$. This is the Brauer--Severi triviality criterion: a Brauer--Severi variety of dimension $1$ is $\mathbb P^1$ over its base if and only if it has a rational point. The genus-$0$ identification on the curve side uses $h^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2$ (Riemann--Roch) to produce a degree-$1$ morphism $C \to \mathbb P^1_k$, which is then an isomorphism by degree-and-dimension.
327	
328	    \item[\textbf{(C.2)}] \emph{Rigidity for morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety.} We state the rigidity result over the algebraic closure $\bar k$, since the protected signature of Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} does not assume $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ and the genus-$0$ identification $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ is only available after passing to $\bar k$ (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexamples to a $k$-side identification without a rational point). The conclusion over $k$ --- needed only in the sub-case $C(k) \neq \emptyset$, where Sub-step~C.1 gives $C \cong \mathbb P^1_k$ already over $k$ --- then follows by Galois descent (Sub-step~C.2.f).
329	
330	    \begin{itemize}
331	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.a)}] \emph{Statement (over $\bar k$).} Let $A$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$, with identity element $\eta_A \in A(\bar k)$. Let
332	      \[
333	        f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \longrightarrow A
334	      \]
335	      be a morphism of $\bar k$-schemes such that $f(p) = \eta_A$ for some closed point $p \in \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}(\bar k)$. Then $f$ factors as the composite $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A$; equivalently, $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$.
336	
337	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.b)}] \emph{Reduction to the project's rigidity lemma.} Let $c \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ denote the constant morphism at $\eta_A$ --- the unique $\bar k$-morphism factoring through the closed-point section $\eta_A \colon \Spec \bar k \hookrightarrow A$. The conclusion $f = c$ follows from Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} applied to $g_1 := f$, $g_2 := c$, $X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, $Y := A$, once we produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $U.\iota \circ f = U.\iota \circ c$ as scheme morphisms. (After the iter-125 refactor, the cited theorem is stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated; the source-side group-object decoration that the earlier formulation carried has been dropped. $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ satisfies the refactored hypotheses trivially --- it is smooth, hence reduced; it is geometrically irreducible over $\bar k$; and $A$ is proper hence separated over $\bar k$.) The reduction therefore amounts to: produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $f$ and $c$ agree as scheme morphisms; we will in fact take $U = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
338	
339	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.c)}] \emph{Image dimension argument.} The morphism $f$ goes from a proper geometrically integral $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $1$ to a smooth proper $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $g := \dim_{\bar k} A$. The set-theoretic image $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) \subseteq A$ is an irreducible closed subset of $A$ (the continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper map is irreducible and closed) of dimension at most $1$. If $g = 0$ then $A = \Spec \bar k$ and the conclusion is vacuous: the unique $\bar k$-morphism to $\Spec \bar k$ is the constant morphism. Henceforth assume $g \geq 1$, in which case the image is either a single point (the desired conclusion) or a one-dimensional irreducible proper closed subscheme of $A$ --- an irreducible rational curve in $A$.
340	
341	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.d)}] \emph{Key classical input: proper rational curves on an abelian variety are constant.} The remaining case --- image of dimension exactly $1$ --- is ruled out by the following classical fact (Mumford, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Chapter~II~\S4, Proposition):
342	
343	      \begin{quote}
344	        \emph{Let $A$ be an abelian variety of positive dimension over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$. Then every morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant.}
345	      \end{quote}
346	
347	      Two standard proofs are available; both unwind a few layers of upstream Mathlib infrastructure but are routine once the input lemmas are present.
348	      \begin{itemize}
349	        \item \emph{Via the dual abelian variety.} Pullback of line bundles along $f$ induces a homomorphism of group schemes $f^{\vee} \colon A^{\vee} \to \Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$, where $A^{\vee}$ is the dual abelian variety (the moduli space of degree-zero line bundles on $A$). The Picard group of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ has trivial degree-zero component, $\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0$, because every line bundle on $\mathbb P^1$ of degree $0$ is isomorphic to $\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}$ (Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, II.6 and II.7). Hence $f^{\vee} = 0$. By autoduality of abelian varieties ($A^{\vee\vee} = A$ canonically, double duality of the Poincar\'e line bundle), the zero of $f^{\vee}$ implies that $f$ itself is constant.
350	
351	        \item \emph{Via the cotangent bundle.} The cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ of an abelian variety is a trivial $\mathcal O_A$-module: the tangent bundle of a group scheme over a field is generated globally by translates of the Lie algebra at the identity, hence is trivial, hence so is its dual. Pulling back along $f$ yields a morphism of locally free sheaves $f^{*} \Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}$, whose global sections form a $\bar k$-linear map
352	        \[
353	          H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}\bigr) \longrightarrow H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}\bigr) = H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(-2)\bigr) = 0.
354	        \]
355	        Triviality of $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ means the left-hand side is a non-zero $\bar k$-vector space (of dimension $g$); the only way the map can land in zero is for it to be the zero map at every fibre, i.e.\ for the differential $df$ to vanish identically on $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. Smoothness of $A$ over $\bar k$ together with the vanishing of $df$ forces $f$ to be a constant morphism on the geometric points and hence (by reduced-source / separated-target, see C.2.e) as a scheme morphism.
356	      \end{itemize}
357	      Either proof yields: $f$ is constant. The pointed condition $f(p) = \eta_A$ then identifies the constant value, so $f = c$ on underlying topological points.
358	
359	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.e)}] \emph{Promoting set-level equality to scheme equality.} The set-level equality $f = c$ on underlying topological points (established in C.2.d) promotes to scheme-morphism equality on any non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via the standard reduced-source / separated-target argument: $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ is reduced (smooth implies regular, regular implies reduced) and $A$ is separated over $\bar k$ (proper implies separated), so a $\bar k$-morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is determined by its values on the underlying topological space. In particular, $f|_U = c|_U$ as scheme morphisms for the open $U := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ itself, and the reduction of C.2.b gives $f = c$ as scheme morphisms.
360	
361	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.f)}] \emph{[DROPPED iter-127.] Galois descent to $k$ --- no longer needed.} Prior strategy iterations routed the conclusion of C.2.d through the algebraic closure $\bar k$ and then descended morphism equality back to $k$ via faithful flatness of $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (M2.c in STRATEGY.md, $\sim 4$--$8$ iter / $300$--$500$ LOC). The iter-127 over-k analogist (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md}) verified that the three active pieces of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (cotangent triviality via the functorial shear iso $(a, b) \mapsto (a, a \cdot b)$; \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} as a $k$-agnostic Mathlib typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$) all build directly over an arbitrary base field $k$ with no algebraic-closure or perfect-base hypothesis. The keystone of C.2.d is therefore established over $k$ directly, and \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (the project's named rigidity declaration; see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}) is invoked in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch of \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} with the supplied $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ feeding the pointing hypothesis. No base-change-and-descent step is performed. Galois descent of morphism equality is consequently \textbf{not} a Mathlib gap that the project needs to fill.
362	
363	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.g)}] \emph{Mathlib gap statement (iter-127 over-k inventory).} The keystone fact of Sub-step~C.2.d --- that every morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve over a base field $k$ to a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $k$ is constant --- is \emph{not} present in current Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) in any form usable here. The project records it as the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (the filename and Lean identifier still carry the iter-126 over-$\bar k$ name, but the signature is already $k$-agnostic --- \texttt{[Field kbar]}, no \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} --- and the prose framing has shifted to the over-k reading; a low-priority rename \texttt{rigidity\_over\_kbar} $\to$ \texttt{rigidity\_over\_k} is scheduled for iter-128+). The keystone decomposes into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)--(iii): (i) cotangent triviality of a group scheme over an arbitrary base field $k$ via the functorial shear iso (no $\bar k$-rational-point lifting); (ii) scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec k$ via the $k$-agnostic \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass plus the iter-125-refactored \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}; (iii) characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ (no perfect-base / alg-closure required). Each is an upstream Mathlib gap-fill in its own right (see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}). When $g = \dim A = 0$ the statement is vacuous and the declaration's conclusion is automatic. The reduction step C.2.b reuses the project's already-formalised Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (after the iter-125 refactor, this is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated); the promotion step C.2.e uses standard reduced-source / separated-target lemmas, partially in Mathlib. The Galois-descent gap previously associated with C.2.f (M2.c and its companion M2.c.aux \texttt{geomIrred.exists\_kalg\_pt}) is \textbf{eliminated} under the iter-127 over-k commitment: rigidity is established directly over $k$, so no base-change-and-descent step appears anywhere in the M2 critical path. Total savings: $500$--$900$ LOC / $7$--$13$ iter relative to the prior over-$\bar k$-then-descend plan.
364	    \end{itemize}
365	
366	    \item[\textbf{(C.3)}] \emph{The trivial witness.} Take $J := \Spec k$, the terminal $k$-scheme. The group-object structure on $\Spec k$ is trivial; it is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of relative dimension $0 = g(C)$. The unique morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to \Spec k$ sends $P$ to the (unique) point. By Sub-step C.2 every $f \colon C \to A$ with $f(P) = \eta_A$ is constant at $\eta_A$, hence factors uniquely through the unique morphism $g \colon \Spec k \to A$ sending the point to $\eta_A$; uniqueness of $g$ follows because $\Spec k \to A$ is determined by its value on the unique $k$-rational point of $\Spec k$.
367	  \end{itemize}
368	
369	  \emph{Mathlib status for the genus-$0$ sub-case.}
370	  \begin{itemize}
371	    \item Sub-step C.1 requires the genus-$0$ identification on the curve side, which in turn requires Serre duality / the basic Riemann--Roch infrastructure that this project has explicitly trimmed (see the project's Phase~C exit policy). Mathlib has partial $\mathbb P^1$-theory but the curve-side genus-$0$ identification is missing.
372	    \item Sub-step C.2 reduces, via the project's already-formalised rigidity lemma (Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} of Chapter~\ref{chap:Rigidity}), to the classical fact that proper rational curves on a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over an arbitrary base field $k$ are constant (the keystone of Sub-step~C.2.d, packaged as \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}). This classical fact is not in Mathlib in the form needed here; see Sub-step~C.2.g for the explicit gap statement and the over-k inventory of pieces (i)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile. Galois descent of morphism equality is \textbf{no longer} a Mathlib prerequisite under the iter-127 over-k commitment: Sub-step~C.2.f is DROPPED (see above).
373	    \item Sub-step C.3 is unconditional once C.1 and C.2 are in place.
374	  \end{itemize}

389	\subsection{The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence}
390	\label{sec:genusZeroWitness}
391	
392	The genus-$0$ sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} admits an explicit witness construction at the Lean level, packaged separately from the general existence statement so that the genus-$0$ branch can be discharged independently once the shared cotangent-vanishing pile lands (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii)).
393	
394	\begin{theorem}\leanok
395	[Genus-$0$ Albanese witness]
396	  \label{def:genusZeroWitness}
397	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}
398	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar, def:genus}
399	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is $\Spec k$ (the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $0$, properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$ field encodes the Albanese universal property for every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$.
400	\end{theorem}
401	
402	\begin{proof}
403	  \uses{thm:rigidity_over_kbar, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:IsAlbanese, def:JacobianWitness, def:genus}
404	  \leanok
405	  We construct the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness}.
406	
407	  \paragraph{Underlying scheme.} Take $J := \mathbf{1}$, the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, which on underlying schemes is $\Spec k$ viewed as a $k$-scheme via the identity structure morphism.
408	
409	  \paragraph{Group-object structure, properness, smoothness, geometric irreducibility.} The terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ of any cartesian-monoidal category carries the (unique) trivial group-object structure: multiplication and inverse are the unique morphisms to $\mathbf{1}$, and the identity element is the identity morphism. The structure morphism $\mathbf{1}.\mathtt{hom} = \mathrm{id}_{\Spec k}$ is proper (the identity is a closed immersion, in particular proper), smooth (the identity is unramified and flat, hence smooth), and geometrically irreducible. The geometric-irreducibility instance is the project-local helper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.geometricallyIrreducible\_id\_Spec} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:120}--\texttt{126}), which derives geometric irreducibility of the identity on $\Spec k$ from the fact that, on the geometric fibre $\Spec K$ over any field extension $k \to K$, the second projection of the pullback square is an isomorphism, and $\Spec K$ is irreducible.
410	
411	  \paragraph{Smoothness of relative dimension $\genus\,C$.} The smoothness witness $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is required at relative dimension $\genus\,C$. The hypothesis $\genus\,C = 0$ rewrites the target relative dimension to $0$, after which the goal reduces to $\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 0\ (\mathrm{id}_{\Spec k})$. This is a Mathlib instance: the identity morphism on any base is smooth of relative dimension $0$.
412	
413	  \paragraph{The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field.} For every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$, we exhibit an \cref{def:IsAlbanese} term for the triple $(C, P, J)$.
414	
415	  The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map $\alpha := \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \colon C \to \mathbf{1}$ (the structure morphism of $C$ as a $k$-scheme, factored through the terminal object). The pointed condition $P \circ \alpha = \eta_J = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}$ holds automatically: both sides are morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, and the terminal object has a unique endomorphism.
416	
417	  For the universal factorisation: given a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $A$ over $k$ with identity element $\eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$ and a morphism $f \colon C \to A$ with $P \circ f = \eta_A$, we must exhibit a unique morphism $g \colon J \to A$ such that $f = \alpha \circ g$. \emph{Existence}: take $g := \eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$. Then $\alpha \circ g = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$, which equals $f$ precisely when $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. This is the content of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (the project's named rigidity declaration of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, established directly over the base field $k$ per the iter-127 over-k commitment): the supplied marked point $P \in C(k)$ feeds the pointing hypothesis, and the rigidity conclusion is exactly $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$ as scheme morphisms. \emph{Uniqueness}: any two morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to A$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ agreeing on $P \circ (-) = \eta_A$ are equal by the universal property of the terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ as a category (a morphism out of $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ is determined by where it sends the unique generator, here pinned to $\eta_A$).
418	
419	  \paragraph{Vacuity for the $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch.} When no $k$-rational point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$ exists (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the universal quantifier $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\,C\,P\,J$ is vacuously satisfied at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall$ over an empty type is trivially true. The construction handles this branch implicitly without any explicit case split.
420	
421	  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (M2.a), which in turn depends on pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}). Under the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot (and the iter-145 Wave-2 chart-algebra envelope), the revised trajectory is: iter-146+ chart-algebra piece (ii) sub-piece prover lane; iter-149+ M2.a body closure (\cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}); iter-151+ M2.b body closure (\cref{def:genusZeroWitness}). Earliest realistic body-closure iteration: \textbf{iter-151+ (revised iter-145; under the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot)}.
422	\end{proof}
423	
424	\subsection{The positive-genus arm of the witness existence}
425	\label{sec:positiveGenusWitness}
426	
427	The positive-genus sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} is packaged separately from the genus-$0$ arm so that the two arms can be closed independently. Whereas the genus-$0$ arm \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} routes through the rigidity declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, the positive-genus arm requires the full Albanese construction via Route A (Picard scheme via FGA --- Hilbert/Quot infrastructure plus FGA representability) of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, which is the committed M3 route per the iter-144 disposition in STRATEGY.md \S~M3. Route B (symmetric powers + Stein factorisation) is a historical alternative not pursued by the project.
428	
429	\begin{theorem}\leanok
430	[Positive-genus Albanese witness]
431	  \label{def:positiveGenusWitness}
432	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness}
433	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, def:genus, def:IsAlbanese}
434	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) \ge 1$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is the Albanese variety of $C$ (classically the identity component of the Picard scheme $\mathrm{Pic}^0_{C/k}$, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel--Jacobi morphism on $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$). The full $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}$ data (group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $g$, properness, geometric irreducibility, and Albanese universal property uniformly over $k$-rational marked points) is supplied by the Route A construction (per \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route A; Route B is documented as a historical alternative not pursued).
435	\end{theorem}
436	
437	\begin{proof}
438	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, def:genus, def:IsAlbanese, thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}
439	  \leanok
440	  This is the M3 arm of the witness existence. M3 is \textbf{committed to Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA} (per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition; $\sim 6500$ LOC midpoint per the iter-123 audit \texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}). The autonomous loop writes the $\sim 6500$-LOC Mathlib material directly in-tree; upstream PR extraction is an OPTIONAL downstream lift, not a project deliverable. The committed route produces the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness} in positive genus via the following Mathlib-prerequisite path:
441	  \begin{itemize}
442	    \item \emph{Route A: Hilbert/Quot infrastructure plus FGA representability of $\Pic_{C/k}$.} See \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route A for the four-step decomposition (A.1 relative Picard functor; A.2 representability; A.3 identity component; A.4 Abel--Jacobi universal morphism). The smallest entry point into Route A is the \texttt{RelativeSpec} functor (A.1 prerequisite; $\sim 700$--$1100$ LOC) per the iter-123 audit.
443	  \end{itemize}
444	  Route B (symmetric powers $C^{(g)} := C^g/S_g$ + Stein factorisation, $\sim 9000$ LOC midpoint per the iter-123 audit) is documented in \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route B as a historical alternative not pursued by the project.
445	
446	  M3 sits behind M2 critical-path during the M2 wait window; whether to start Route A scaffolding (smallest entry: \texttt{RelativeSpec} functor at $\sim 700$--$1100$ LOC) in parallel with M2 or strictly after M2 closes is a planner-level scheduling call. Existing iter-150 mathlib-analogist scheduling trigger: dispatched if cumulative M2.body-pile LOC exceeds $925$ LOC without piece (i.b) closing, OR if M2.a body closure has not landed by iter-160.
447	
448	  The scaffold \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness} (landed iter-134) is load-bearing iter-135 onward for the genus-stratified body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (iter-135 refactor): the body case-splits $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ and delegates to \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} on the $\neg(\genus\,C = 0)$ arm via \texttt{Nat.pos\_of\_ne\_zero}. The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness} is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is contingent on Route A of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} being prosecuted in full.
449	\end{proof}
450	
451	\section{Implementation route via the Albanese functor}
452	
453	Because the full Picard-scheme representability theorem is not available in current Mathlib, the formal construction proceeds in two layers:
454	
455	\begin{enumerate}
456	  \item \textbf{Layer I --- direct definition.} The Jacobian of $C$ is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of the Albanese witness provided by Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, carrying the four properties of Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred} (each obtained by projection from the witness). The genus-$0$ case is included: the witness's universal property in that case carries the rigidity content for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme. Per the iter-127 over-k commitment (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md}), this rigidity is established directly over the base field $k$ via the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} and the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii); no base-change to $\bar k$ and no Galois descent of morphism equality enters. The genus-$0$ arm is packaged as the explicit Lean construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}: the underlying scheme is the terminal object $\Spec k$, the universal morphism in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch is the unique terminal map \texttt{toUnit}\,$C$, and the universal factorisation reduces to \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}.
457	
458	  \item \textbf{Layer II --- Abel--Jacobi compatibility.} Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} introduces the Abel--Jacobi morphism $C \to \Jac(C)$ and shows that it is universal among morphisms from $C$ to abelian varieties.
459	\end{enumerate}

95	standard-smooth `k`-algebra of relative dimension `n`, if `b : B` satisfies
96	`D b = 0` in `Ω_{B/k}` then `b ∈ range (algebraMap k B)`. This is the

108	inline. The structured `sorry` still concentrates at (BR.5) — the
109	char-0 joint-kernel-of-coordinate-derivations equals

111	fill of the bridge (~80–150 LOC of iter-150+ project work).

114	Cartier-direction alternative path documented in the iter-148 docstring

116	hypothesis. A future char-p formalisation would either branch on

118	
119	Reverse inclusion: `_hRev` records that `D ∘ algebraMap k B = 0` so the

123	theorem KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero
124	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [CharZero k]
125	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
126	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
127	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
128	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
129	  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
130	  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
131	  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
132	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
133	    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
134	  -- (BR.2) Free-module structure on `Ω[B⁄k]` from the standard-smooth
135	  -- chart hypothesis: `Module.Free B Ω[B⁄k]` of rank `n`. The relative-
136	  -- dimension class is a `Prop`, not registered as an instance for
137	  -- `IsStandardSmooth`, so dispatch through the named theorem.
138	  haveI _hStdSm : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth k B :=
139	    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
140	  haveI _hFree : Module.Free B (Ω[B⁄k]) :=
141	    Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	  -- (BR.3) Coordinate-derivation construction. For a chosen basis
143	  -- `{dx_i}_{i : ChooseBasisIndex B Ω[B⁄k]}` of `Ω[B⁄k]`, each B-linear
144	  -- coordinate functional `basis.coord i : Ω[B⁄k] →ₗ[B] B` composes with
145	  -- the universal Kähler derivation `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` (via
146	  -- `LinearMap.compDer`) to produce a k-derivation
147	  -- `∂_i : Derivation k B B`. Since `D b = 0` (hypothesis), each
148	  -- `∂_i b = (basis.coord i) (D b) = (basis.coord i) 0 = 0` (record
149	  -- this for the (BR.5) consumer).
150	  let _basis := Module.Free.chooseBasis B (Ω[B⁄k])
151	  have _hCoordVanish : ∀ i, ((_basis.coord i).compDer
152	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B)) b = 0 := by
153	    intro i
154	    -- `∂_i b = (basis.coord i) (D b) = (basis.coord i) 0 = 0` via the
155	    -- `LinearMap.compDer` coe-unfolding and `LinearMap.map_zero`.
156	    simp [Derivation.coe_comp, hDb]
157	  -- (BR.4) `Differential B` instance: each `∂_i : Derivation k B B`
158	  -- restricts to a `Derivation ℤ B B` via `Derivation.restrictScalars`,
159	  -- which is the witness for the `Differential B` structure. Multiple
160	  -- coordinate `Differential B` instances are mutually incompatible
161	  -- (Lean's typeclass synthesis picks one); we keep this as an
162	  -- explicit `let` rather than registering it globally.
163	  --
164	  -- (BR.5) JOINT-KERNEL COLLAPSE — the substantive remaining gap.
165	  -- Mathlib's `Differential.ContainConstants A B` typeclass packages
166	  -- the property "`b' = 0 ⇒ b ∈ range (algebraMap A B)`" for a SINGLE
167	  -- derivation `b' : B → B`. For our setup, `∂_1 b = 0` alone is
168	  -- INSUFFICIENT — the kernel of a single coordinate derivation is
169	  -- strictly larger than `range (algebraMap k B)`. For instance, in
170	  -- `B = k[x_1, x_2]` with `∂_1 = ∂/∂x_1`, `ker ∂_1 = k[x_2] ⊋ k`.
171	  --
172	  -- The correct collapse in char 0 + standard smooth uses the JOINT
173	  -- kernel `⋂_i ker(∂_i) = range (algebraMap k B)`. The argument
174	  -- structure: induct on the standard-smooth presentation
175	  -- `B ≅ k[x_1, …, x_n, y_1, …, y_m] / (f_1, …, f_m)` with invertible
176	  -- Jacobian `det(∂f_i/∂y_j)`; the Jacobian condition expresses each
177	  -- `dy_j` as a `B`-linear combination of the `dx_i`'s. At each
178	  -- inductive step, a single `∂_i b = 0` confines `b` to a smaller
179	  -- `k`-subalgebra; after `n` steps the intersection collapses to
180	  -- `range (algebraMap k B)`. In characteristic 0 the integer
181	  -- coefficients in the polynomial expansion of `b` never vanish,
182	  -- so each per-coordinate kernel collapses to "no `x_i`-dependence"
183	  -- (Stacks Tag 07F4 in the smooth case, with the char-0 collapse
184	  -- making the kernel exactly the constants).
185	  --
186	  -- This induction is the substantive iter-150+ Mathlib-PR-grade work
187	  -- (~40–80 LOC) that the project's `Differential.ContainConstants`
188	  -- bridge requires as an instance synthesis. The (BR.1)–(BR.4)
189	  -- scaffolding above lands the signature inflation, the freeness
190	  -- of `Ω[B⁄k]`, and the coordinate-derivation extraction — leaving
191	  -- only (BR.5) joint-kernel collapse as the residual gap. The
192	  -- in-tree continuation point is the `_hCoordVanish` lemma above
193	  -- (a-bridge-from-`D b = 0`-to-`∀ i, ∂_i b = 0`).
194	  sorry

226	    [Smooth (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
227	    [IsReduced C]

# Blueprint Review Report

## Slug

iter150

## Iteration

150

## Top-level summaries

### Incomplete parts

- `RigidityKbar.tex` § "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class decomposition": every load-bearing block is structurally present (chart-algebra (α) `algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product`; chart-algebra (β-core) `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`; the four (S3.*) sub-claims; KDM `KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero`; (β-aux) `constants_integral_over_base_field`; (β-aux-chart) `KaehlerDifferential_constants_in_chart_of_proper_curve`; scheme-level lift `Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero`). No mathematically missing blocks in this section relative to the strategy.

### Proofs lacking detail

- `RigidityKbar.tex` / `lem:S3_pi_2_isPurelyInseparable_of_unique_minPrime_baseChange` proof block (lines 2058–2065): the closure logic conflates two distinct chase steps. Sentence "Its nilradical reduction $(F \otimes_k \bar k) / \mathrm{Nil}$ has the same prime spectrum and, with the unique-minimal-prime hypothesis, is an Artinian integral domain finite over $\bar k$ --- hence a field finite over $\bar k$" elides the step that the nilradical reduction is a *product* of Artinian local domains, then uses unique-min-prime to force a single factor. The blueprint proof in (S3.sep.2) (`lem:S3_sep_2_geom_reduced_finite_field_ext_is_separable`, lines 2010–2017) does articulate the product-of-fields decomposition explicitly; the (S3.pi.2) block should mirror that articulation. Soon-severity nudge for the writer: add one sentence on "Artinian + unique minimal prime ⇒ local" (the prover-side comment at `ChartAlgebraS3.lean:291–295` makes this explicit; the blueprint should match).
- `RigidityKbar.tex` / `lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` proof Step 3 (lines 1952–1964): "chart-uniform existence is automatic" elides why the standard-smooth presentation of $\Omega_{A/k}$ on $W$ propagates to all preimages of $W$ in $C$ with the *same* generators $b_1, \ldots, b_g$. A reader would expect a short justification (presumably: pull the chart presentation back along $f$, and use that the basis $\{db_i\}$ is preserved by base change of Kähler modules). One sentence would suffice.

### Lean difficulty quality

- `RigidityKbar.tex` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Gamma_baseChange_iso_tensor_of_proper}` (line 2024): the prover-side signature in `ChartAlgebraS3.lean:194` packages the conclusion as `Nonempty (TensorProduct k Γ K ≃ₐ[K] Γ(X_K))`.
